content: I will run a node command to specifically parse out the console logs that were captured by the browser subagent, without loading the entire JSONL step data.

args.CommandLine: node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const content = fs.readFileSync('C:/Users/HP/.gemini/antigravity-ide/brain/23ca7c28-52ef-4734-8837-5dc8a6898aa1/.system_generated/logs/transcript_full.jsonl', 'utf8'); const regex = /\"type\":\"BROWSER_SUBAGENT\"[^\n]+/g; let match; while ((match = regex.exec(content)) !== null) { const step = JSON.parse(match[0]); if (step.content && step.content.includes('capture_browser_console_logs')) { console.log('================== STEP LOGS =================='); console.log(step.content); } }"
